title: Arkadiankadun siltaremontti aiheuttaa muutoksia liikennejärjestelyihin Luonnontieteellisen museon lähistöllä
content:
Renewal of Arkadiankatu Bridge will cause changes to traffic arrangements near the Natural History Museum

johanemi@helsinki.fi Wed, 07/01/2026 - 08:40
The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

miur@helsinki.fi Tue, 21/04/2026 - 13:39

title: Maidontuotannon hiilijalanjälki mahdollisesti oletettua suurempi
content:
Milk production carbon footprint may be larger than thought

heheiki@helsinki.fi Tue, 21/04/2026 - 08:20
Livestock farming causes a significant share of the world’s greenhouse gas emissions. According to a new study, the carbon footprint of milk may be considerably larger than commonly calculated when carbon released from the soil is also considered.

title: New Fellows selected to the Helsinki Collegium for Advanced Studies
content:
New Fellows selected to the Helsinki Collegium for Advanced Studies

visiiri@helsinki.fi Mon, 20/04/2026 - 12:24
The HCAS is pleased to announce the selection of new fellows. In the fall of 2026, the HCAS welcomes 12 new Core Fellows, 6 Kone Foundation Visiting Fellows (East & South), 1 Postdoctoral Fellow in the Arts, and 1 HCAS-RJ Fellow. The Jane and Aatos Erkko Professor of 2026–27 is Anne McCants.

title: HIKET session at the 5th ACCC Impact Week
content:
HIKET session at the 5th ACCC Impact Week

sxsilven@helsinki.fi Mon, 20/04/2026 - 12:10
At the 5th ACCC Impact Week, the HIKET session focused on strengthening the knowledge base of carbon sinks, enabling more robust, evidence-based LULUCF-related decision-making.

title: Suomen Tiedeseuran suuri palkinto 2026 professori Tuomas Heikkilälle
content:
Grand Prize of the Finnish Society of Sciences and Letters 2026 awarded to Professor Tuomas Heikkilä

pettergr@helsinki.fi Mon, 20/04/2026 - 11:34
Professor Tuomas Heikkilä of the University of Helsinki has been awarded the Grand Prize of the Finnish Society of Sciences and Letters – the Professor E. J. Nyström’s Prize 2026, worth €50,000 – in recognition of his versatile and pioneering research in medieval studies.
